title: Understanding the System
text: The system consists of a rotating disk and a bug. Initially, the bug is at the edge of the disk, and then it moves to the center. How does this movement affect the system's kinetic energy?

title: Conservation of Angular Momentum
text: Since there are no external torques acting on the system, the angular momentum is conserved. How does this principle help in determining the change in kinetic energy?

title: Initial and Final Moments of Inertia
text: Calculate the initial and final moments of inertia of the system. Remember, the moment of inertia of the disk is $$\frac{1}{2} M R^2$$, and the bug contributes $$m r^2$$ to the moment of inertia when it is at a distance $$r$$ from the axis.

title: Initial and Final Angular Velocities
text: Using the conservation of angular momentum, determine the initial and final angular velocities of the system. The initial angular velocity is given as $$10.0$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.

title: Calculating Kinetic Energy
text: The kinetic energy of a rotating object is given by $$\frac{1}{2} I {\omega}^2$$, where I is the moment of inertia and omega is the angular velocity. Calculate the initial and final kinetic energies of the system.

title: Change in Kinetic Energy
text: Find the difference between the final and initial kinetic energies to determine the change in kinetic energy of the system.
